meaning: <德>n.(第二次世界大战期间纳粹德国的)反间谍机关
examples: The book alleges that in 1940, Chanel was recruited into the Abwehr - her nom DE guerre borrowed from another of her lovers, the Duke of Westminster.
For all the Germans' much-vaunted efficiency in other enterprises, their wartime spy organisation, the Abwehr, was a dud.
